---
title: |
  Machine Learning for Characterization and Processing<br>Unit 3: Data quality, labels, and leakage
subtitle: "AI 4 Materials / KI-Materialtechnologie"
author:
  - name: Prof. Dr. Philipp Pelz
    affiliation:
      - FAU Erlangen-Nürnberg
bibliography: ref.bib
execute:
  eval: false
format:
  revealjs:
    width: 1920
    height: 1080
    template-partials:
      - title-slide.html
    css: custom.css
    theme: custom.scss
    slide-number: c/t
    logo: "eclipse_logo_small.png"
    footer: "© Philipp Pelz - ML for Characterization and Processing"
---

## 01. The Quality Crisis in Scientific ML

- **Garbage In, Garbage Out**: An algorithm is only as good as the data it sees.
- In materials science, data is expensive, so we tend to use everything we have—even the "garbage".
- **The Reproducibility Crisis**: Many published models fail when applied to new samples.

## 02. Data Cleaning: The "Fix at Source" Principle

- Digital cleaning (interpolating NaNs, smoothing) is a bandage.
- **The Ideal**: Improve the measurement chain (sensors, shielding, prep).
- Neuer Ch 3: Preprocessing is essential but cannot replace high-quality raw data.

## 03. Handling Missing Data (Neuer Ch 3)

- **Deletion**: Removing rows/columns with NaNs (wasteful for small data).
- **Imputation**: Filling in the blanks.
  - Linear/Spline interpolation for time-series.
  - Mean/Median/Mode imputation (dangerous for multi-modal data).
  - Physics-based constraints: Filling based on conservation laws.

## 04. Systematic Cleaning Pipeline

1. **Detection**: Identifying outliers and NaNs.
2. **Diagnosis**: Is the outlier physical (e.g., rare defect) or an artifact (e.g., cosmic ray)?
3. **Treatment**: Deleting, capping, or imputing.
4. **Documentation**: Always report how much data was altered!

## 05. Normalization and Scaling

- **Min-Max Scaling**: $[0, 1]$. Brittle to outliers.
- **Standardization (Z-score)**: Mean $0$, Variance $1$.
- **Robust Scaler**: Using Interquartile Range (IQR). Best for noisy lab data.
- *Critical Rule*: Scalers must be "fit" on training data only and "applied" to test data.

---

## 06. The Annotation Problem

- Who provides the "Ground Truth"?
- In microscopy, labeling is manual and tedious (e.g., drawing grain boundaries).
- **Label Uncertainty**: If two experts disagree, which one is "right"?

## 07. Inter-Annotator Variance

- Different experts segment the same micrograph differently.
- This variance sets an **Upper Bound** on meaningful model performance.
- If humans only agree 85% of the time, a model claiming 99% accuracy is likely overfitting to one person's bias.

## 08. Active Learning: Smart Annotation

- Instead of labeling everything, the model asks: "Label this specific image because I am most uncertain about it."
- Reduces the manual burden while focusing on the "hard" physical cases.

---

## 09. Data Leakage: The Silent Killer

- **Definition**: When information from the future (test set) "leaks" into the past (training set).
- It leads to over-optimistic results that vanish during real deployment.
- "If your accuracy is too good to be true, it probably is."

## 10. Spatial Leakage in Materials

- A sample is cut into 100 patches.
- 80 patches go to Train, 20 to Test.
- **The Problem**: Patches from the same physical sample are highly correlated.
- *Result*: The model "recognizes" the specific sample instead of learning general physics.
- **Solution**: Split by **Sample ID** or **Batch ID**.

## 11. Temporal Leakage in Processing

- Predict the outcome of a process based on sensor logs.
- **The Problem**: Using data from $t=50$ min to predict a property measured at $t=10$ min.
- **Solution**: "Walk-forward" validation. Only use data available *at the time of prediction*.

## 12. Preprocessing Leakage

- Calculating the Mean/Standard Deviation of the **entire** dataset before splitting.
- Information about the test set distribution is now embedded in the training features.
- **Solution**: Use Pipeline objects (e.g., Scikit-Learn Pipelines) to encapsulate scaling.

---

## 13. Robust Validation Strategies

- **K-Fold Cross-Validation**: Rotating the test set to get a stable performance estimate.
- **Grouped K-Fold**: Ensuring that all patches from one sample stay in the same fold.
- **Nested Cross-Validation**: One loop for tuning hyperparameters, one for evaluation.

## 14. Metrics for Reliability

- Beyond $R^2$ and Accuracy.
- **Confusion Matrix**: Visualizing where the model gets confused (Sandfeld Ch 18).
- **Residual Analysis**: Plotting $(y_{true} - y_{pred})$ vs $y_{true}$. Are the errors systematic?

## 15. The "Correct" Materials ML Checklist

- [ ] Is my split leakage-safe (Grouped by sample)?
- [ ] Did I fit the scaler only on the training set?
- [ ] Do my labels have an uncertainty estimate?
- [ ] Is my metric sensitive to class imbalance?
- [ ] Have I benchmarked against a simple linear model?

## 16. Recap: Unit 3

- Clean your data, but fix the physics first.
- Human labels are uncertain $\rightarrow$ Quantify the disagreement.
- Beware of Leakage $\rightarrow$ Split by sample, not by pixel.
- Validation must mirror the operational reality.

---

## 17. References & Further Reading

- **Neuer (2024)**: Ch. 3 (Data Preprocessing)
- **Sandfeld (2024)**: Ch. 13 (Validation)
- **Murphy (2012)**: Ch. 1.4 (Overfitting and Model Selection)
- **McClarren (2021)**: Ch. 3 (Evaluation and Metrics)

::: {#refs}
:::
